old_claim.note: Steam Turbine Power Generation provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains from early electrical grids to steam turbine power generation; Pearl Street-style central distribution began in 1882, while the sampled steam-turbine node is dated to Parsons's 1884 turbine work.

source_shape.source_locator: NPS Edison biography section describing the September 1882 Pearl Street commercial power station.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source dates the first commercial power station at Pearl Street to September 1882, before the graph's steam-turbine power-generation node.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The chronology rules out steam_turbine_power_generation as a prerequisite for the first modeled central electrical distribution systems; the grid node remains supported by dynamos, lighting demand, construction, and wiring.

ontology_before: Early electrical distribution depended on steam turbine generation, making an 1882 grid downstream of an 1884 turbine technology.
ontology_after: Early electrical distribution depends on generators, lighting demand, construction, and wiring; steam turbines remain a later generation technology for power plants.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: electrical_grid_early_distribution no longer directly depends on steam_turbine_power_generation.
would_reject_if: The grid node were re-scoped to turbine-era AC power stations rather than early central distribution.
A source showed Pearl Street or earlier commercial central stations used steam turbines as a prerequisite.
The steam_turbine_power_generation node were re-dated before the first commercial central electrical distribution systems.
short_reason: Early grids predate steam turbine power generation.
